summary: Argentina uses 8-digit NCM codes shared across Mercosur countries (same as Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay). NCM is divided into 21 sections and 99 chapters.

sources.name: Argentina Tariff Schedule - World Bank WITS
sources.description: World Bank's tariff database with Argentine schedules

sources.name: AFIP - Federal Administration of Public Income
sources.description: Argentine tax and customs authority maintaining NCM classifications

sources.name: Argentine Customs HS Code Database
sources.description: Argentine HS codes and VAT rates

sources.name: SEAIR HS Code Database
sources.description: Argentine HS codes and trade data

mercosur_alignment.note: First 6 digits are Harmonized System (HS) standard. Digits 7-8 are Mercosur-specific

tariff_rates.note: May vary based on product category and origin

next_steps: Access World Bank WITS for Argentine tariff schedules
Contact AFIP for official NCM database
notes: Argentina uses same NCM system as Brazil. AFIP is official authority. Data available through World Bank WITS and commercial databases.
